title: Verilator internal fault in unique case
body:
Hi,

Verilator is throwing an internal fault on a `unique case` statement. Found in `5.004` but exists on current master and not in previous `v4.2xx`. Something to do with all enumerations being in the `unique case` as it doesn't crash if a case item or the `unique` is removed. I'm afraid I haven't got beyond looking at a stack trace (which looked similar to #3848) as it's taken me a fair while to extract the test case from a large codebase so wanted to report.

```
test_regress/t/t_enum_case.pl 
======================================================================
dist/t_enum_case: ==================================================
-Skip: dist/t_enum_case: scenario 'dist' not enabled for test
dist/t_enum_case: -Skip: Skip: scenario 'dist' not enabled for test
==SUMMARY: Passed 0  Failed 0  Time 0:00
======================================================================
vlt/t_enum_case: ==================================================
	perl /a/work/richardp/sili/20230110-verilator/verilator/test_regress/../bin/verilator --prefix Vt_enum_case ../obj_vlt/t_enum_case/Vt_enum_case__main.cpp --exe --make gmake --x-assign unique -cc -Mdir obj_vlt/t_enum_case --fdedup --debug-check --comp-limit-members 10 -Wno-CASEINCOMPLETE --clk clk  -f input.vc +define+TEST_OBJ_DIR=obj_vlt/t_enum_case +define+TEST_DUMPFILE=obj_vlt/t_enum_case/simx.vcd t/t_enum_case.v    > obj_vlt/t_enum_case/vlt_compile.log
%Error: Verilator internal fault, sorry. Suggest trying --debug --gdbbt
%Error: Command Failed ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; exec /a/work/richardp/sili/20230110-verilator/verilator/test_regress/../bin/verilator_bin --prefix Vt_enum_case ../obj_vlt/t_enum_case/Vt_enum_case__main.cpp --exe --make gmake --x-assign unique -cc -Mdir obj_vlt/t_enum_case --fdedup --debug-check --comp-limit-members 10 -Wno-CASEINCOMPLETE --clk clk -f input.vc +define+TEST_OBJ_DIR=obj_vlt/t_enum_case +define+TEST_DUMPFILE=obj_vlt/t_enum_case/simx.vcd t/t_enum_case.v
%Warning: vlt/t_enum_case: Exec of perl failed: %Error: Verilator internal fault, sorry. Suggest trying --debug --gdbbt

vlt/t_enum_case: %Error: Exec of perl failed: %Error: Verilator internal fault, sorry. Suggest trying --debug --gdbbt
vlt/t_enum_case: FAILED: Exec of perl failed: %Error: Verilator internal fault, sorry. Suggest trying --debug --gdbbt
==SUMMARY: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:00
==SUMMARY: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:00

======================================================================
	#vlt/t_enum_case: %Error: Exec of perl failed: %Error: Verilator internal fault, sorry. Suggest trying --debug --gdbbt
		make && test_regress/t/t_enum_case.pl  --vlt
TESTS DONE, FAILED: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:00
```

The below testcase run on `CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009 (Core)` compiled with `clang version 15.0.3`
```
commit 2d6d1235d65ee08c67d7d3fe896903d33fb7d70b (HEAD -> master, origin/master, origin/HEAD)
Author: Larry Doolittle <ldoolitt@recycle.lbl.gov>
Date:   Sun Jan 8 15:17:24 2023 -0800

    Commentary: Spelling (and grammar) fixes (#3846)
```

`test_regress/t/t_enum_case.v`
```
// DESCRIPTION: Verilator: Verilog Test module
//
// This file ONLY is placed under the Creative Commons Public Domain, for
// any use, without warranty, 2009 by Wilson Snyder.
// SPDX-License-Identifier: CC0-1.0

module t (/*AUTOARG*/);

   enum logic [2:0] {
          e0,
          e1,
          e2,
	  e3
        } EN;

   initial begin

      unique case (EN)
          e0 :;
          e1 :;
          e2 :;
          e3 :;
      endcase

      $write("*-* All Finished *-*\n");
      $finish;
   end

endmodule
```
